_meta.purpose: Curated FireRed gate/questline knowledge — the PRIMARY data source for the Questline Deriver (questline.py). Recognise a gate -> derive how to unlock it -> execute as nav goals. GuideSearch is the fallback when this KB is insufficient.
_meta.source: pret/pokefirered disassembly (data/maps/*/scripts.inc, include/constants/flags.h) + Bulbapedia route pages, distilled offline.
_meta.standing_rule: We deliberately store DIRECTIONS + named places + authoritative FLAGS — NOT disasm map NUMBERS. The disasm map_groups.json list-index does NOT equal the runtime SB1 map number: verified 2026-06-28 it gives ROUTE24=(3,42)/VERMILION=(3,25) while LIVE RAM gives Route24=(3,43) (Cerulean's north edge) and the verified city-block has Vermilion=(3,5). Directions (north/south) and flag ids ARE reliable; map numbers are learned LIVE by the world-model as she walks. The Deriver routes via the live world-model + these direction hints, and confirms each step by its success FLAG.
_meta.updated: 2026-07-06 (the data-billing evening: every gate to credits billed; flag ids from include/constants/flags.h)
_meta.id_caveats: FLAG_GOT_SILPH_SCOPE (0x037) and FLAG_GOT_LIFT_KEY (0x036) are HIDE-class flags in the disasm — live-confirm before trusting a 'set' read; Card Key / Secret Key / Gold Teeth are ITEM-BALL pickups with no single GOT flag — their steps carry sets_flag null and must be confirmed by the NEXT gate opening (or a bag read).

FLAG_GOT_SS_TICKET.note: Set when Bill gives the S.S. Ticket. While UNSET, CeruleanCity_OnTransition->BlockExits parks the Slowbro/Lass/Policeman on the south exit (data/maps/CeruleanCity/scripts.inc). OBTAINED 2026-07-05.

FLAG_TEMP_13.note: Visibility flag of the Cerulean cut tree object at (26,32). The tree persists post-ticket; the pre-Cut route south is the burgled-house pass-through (door (30,11) -> garden -> east corridor -> fence crossing (39-40,32)) — GROUND-TRUTHED 2026-07-06.

FLAG_GOT_HM01.note: HM01 Cut — the S.S. Anne captain.

FLAG_GOT_HM02.note: HM02 Fly — the house west on Route 16 (behind a cut tree).

FLAG_GOT_HM03.note: HM03 Surf — the Safari Zone Secret House.

FLAG_GOT_HM04.note: HM04 Strength — the Fuchsia Safari Warden, after returning his Gold Teeth.

FLAG_GOT_HM05.note: HM05 Flash — Oak's aide in the Route 2 east gate (needs >=10 owned species).

FLAG_GOT_BIKE_VOUCHER.note: Vermilion Pokémon Fan Club chairman's story reward.

FLAG_GOT_BICYCLE.note: Cerulean Bike Shop exchange for the voucher.

FLAG_GOT_POKE_FLUTE.note: Mr. Fuji's thanks, top of Pokémon Tower.

FLAG_GOT_TEA.note: The old woman in Celadon Mansion 1F. Opens the Saffron gatehouses (the thirsty guards).

FLAG_WOKE_UP_ROUTE_12_SNORLAX.note: Route 12 Snorlax woken (Poké Flute) — the east coastal road to Fuchsia opens.

FLAG_GOT_SILPH_SCOPE.note: HIDE-class id — live-confirm. From Giovanni in the Rocket Hideout (under Celadon Game Corner).

FLAG_GOT_LIFT_KEY.note: HIDE-class id — live-confirm. Dropped by a grunt in the Hideout; runs the Hideout lift.

FLAG_BADGE05_GET.note: Soul (Koga). Gates Surf field use (source-confirmed GetInteractedWaterScript).

FLAG_BADGE06_GET.note: Marsh (Sabrina). Saffron is Rocket-locked until Silph Co. clears.

FLAG_BADGE07_GET.note: Volcano (Blaine). Cinnabar gym needs the Mansion's Secret Key.

exit_gates._doc: map_key 'g,n' -> { direction|area -> gate }. A gate names the FLAG that must be SET for the exit to open, plus the in-world blocker, so the recogniser can classify a blocked exit as a STORY gate (not a nav bug) and the deriver knows what to unlock. Flag state is read LIVE — a set flag means no gate.

south.blocker: a man and his Slowbro parked across the road (script: CeruleanCity_EventScript_BlockExits)
south.human: the road south out of Cerulean is blocked right now — a guy and his Slowbro won't budge until I've run an errand up north
south.post_flag_note: even with the ticket the direct fence gap holds a CUT tree — the walking route is THROUGH the burgled house (the door the policeman guarded) and around the east side; the door pass-through primitive executes this (proven 2026-07-06)

capabilities._doc: key (a flag/item/HM name, matching Gate.missing) -> how to OBTAIN it. obtain.prereq is another capability key (chains form the questline graph). 'from' is the map the typical encounter is on; 'dir' is the coarse direction to head FROM there (reliable); 'place_name' is the destination; 'gives_cap'/'sets_flag' is the success condition the executor confirms LIVE. No map numbers (see standing_rule).

FLAG_GOT_SS_TICKET.name: the S.S. Ticket

obtain.place_name: Bill's house, past the Nugget Bridge to the north

FLAG_GOT_SS_TICKET.human: the S.S. Ticket — a guy named Bill, in the house past the Nugget Bridge to the north, hands it over

cut.name: HM01 Cut

obtain.place_name: the S.S. Anne, docked at Vermilion's harbour (south) — board with the S.S. Ticket, the captain's cabin is at the bow past the rival fight

cut.human: HM Cut — the seasick captain of the S.S. Anne in Vermilion gives it to you once you've boarded with the S.S. Ticket

flash.name: HM05 Flash

obtain.place_name: the Route 2 east gate house, by Diglett's Cave south of Pewter
obtain.requires_note: he only hands it over once you OWN >=10 species — build the team/dex first

flash.human: HM Flash — Oak's aide in the little gatehouse on Route 2 gives it to anyone who's caught ten kinds of Pokémon. Lights up Rock Tunnel.

fly.name: HM02 Fly

obtain.npc: the girl in the hidden house
obtain.place_name: the lone house on Route 16, behind a small tree west of Celadon (Cut it to reach her)

fly.human: HM Fly — a girl in the hidden house on Route 16, just west of Celadon behind a cuttable tree, teaches your team to fly you between towns

surf.name: HM03 Surf

obtain.place_name: the Secret House deep in the Safari Zone (enter the Safari gate north of Fuchsia's Center; the house is in the far Area 3, past the ponds)
obtain.requires_note: Safari entry costs 500 and runs on a step budget — reaching the far house may take more than one entry

surf.human: HM Surf — the prize of the Safari Zone's Secret House, deep in the back area. Surf opens the sea: Cinnabar, the Seafoam route, and Victory Road's water

strength.name: HM04 Strength

obtain.place_name: the Warden's house on Fuchsia's east side — he wants his GOLD TEETH back first (they're lying in the Safari Zone's far area, an item ball)

strength.human: HM Strength — Fuchsia's Safari Warden trades it for his lost Gold Teeth (they're lying deep in the Safari Zone). Strength shoves the boulders in Victory Road

gold_teeth.name: the Gold Teeth

obtain.place_name: an item ball deep in the Safari Zone (Area 3, near the Secret House)
obtain.confirm_note: no GOT flag — confirm by the Warden accepting them (FLAG_GOT_HM04 sets)

gold_teeth.human: the Warden's Gold Teeth — lying in the deep Safari Zone near the Secret House

bike.name: the Bicycle

obtain.place_name: the Cerulean Bike Shop (the double-door shop on the south row) — trade in the Bike Voucher

bike.human: the Bicycle — the Cerulean Bike Shop hands it over for a Bike Voucher. It makes Cycling Road (the fast road to Fuchsia) rideable

FLAG_GOT_BIKE_VOUCHER.name: the Bike Voucher

obtain.place_name: the Pokémon Fan Club in Vermilion (west row) — sit through the chairman's story about his beloved Rapidash

FLAG_GOT_BIKE_VOUCHER.human: a Bike Voucher — Vermilion's Fan Club chairman gives one to anyone who listens to his whole Rapidash story

silph_scope.name: the Silph Scope

obtain.place_name: the Rocket Hideout under the Celadon Game Corner (the poster in the Game Corner hides the stairs; the Lift Key opens the elevator; Giovanni holds the Scope on B4)
obtain.confirm_note: 0x037 is HIDE-class — if the flag read is unreliable, confirm by the Tower ghost resolving

silph_scope.human: the Silph Scope — Team Rocket's boss keeps it in the hideout under Celadon's Game Corner. It unmasks the ghost haunting Pokémon Tower

FLAG_GOT_POKE_FLUTE.name: the Poké Flute

obtain.place_name: the top of Pokémon Tower in Lavender — rescue Mr. Fuji from Team Rocket (the Silph Scope gets you past the ghost Marowak on floor 6)

FLAG_GOT_POKE_FLUTE.human: the Poké Flute — Mr. Fuji's thank-you for rescuing him from the Tower. It's the only thing that wakes a sleeping Snorlax

FLAG_WOKE_UP_ROUTE_12_SNORLAX.name: waking the Route 12 Snorlax

obtain.place_name: the giant Snorlax asleep across Route 12's bridge road south of Lavender — play the Poké Flute at it (it wakes and ATTACKS: a catchable L30 wild)

FLAG_WOKE_UP_ROUTE_12_SNORLAX.human: the road south of Lavender is blocked by a snoring Snorlax — the Poké Flute wakes it (and it's a heck of a catch if I'm quick)

FLAG_GOT_TEA.name: the Tea

obtain.place_name: Celadon Mansion's ground floor (the big building by the Center) — the old woman hands her Tea to a polite trainer

FLAG_GOT_TEA.human: the Tea — an old lady in Celadon Mansion gives it away; the thirsty guards in Saffron's gatehouses let you through for it (until then, the Underground Paths go AROUND Saffron)

route_notes._doc: Sherpa data for the engine's route layer (inert to the questline recogniser — hints, not gates).
route_notes.cerulean_to_vermilion: Cerulean -> (burgled-house pass-through, proven) -> Route 5 south -> the Route 5 gatehouse guards refuse (thirsty, need Tea) -> use the UNDERGROUND PATH: the lone hut on Route 5's east side, down the stairs, the long tunnel south, up into the Route 6 hut, out to Route 6 -> Vermilion. The door pass-through primitive should discover the huts once multi-hop.
route_notes.celadon_reach: Lavender -> Route 8 west -> the Route 8 gatehouse also refuses pre-Tea -> the SECOND Underground Path (Routes 7-8, entrances near the gatehouses) goes under Saffron east-west -> Route 7 -> Celadon.
route_notes.fuchsia_reach: Two roads: Cycling Road (Route 16/17, needs bike; the Route 16 gate also has a Snorlax with its own wake flag 0x080-class hide id) OR the coastal Routes 12-15 south of Lavender (needs the Route 12 Snorlax woken). Both converge on Fuchsia.
route_notes.saffron_unlock: Tea opens the gatehouses; inside, Silph Co. is Rocket-held — clearing it (Card Key on 5F opens doors; Giovanni #2 on 11F) frees the city and unlocks Sabrina's gym.
route_notes.cinnabar_reach: Surf south from Pallet Town (Route 21) — the shortest sea road. The gym is locked; the Secret Key (an item ball in the burned Mansion's basement) opens it.
route_notes.viridian_gym: Closed until badge 7; Giovanni again, ground types.
route_notes.victory_road: Route 22 -> 23 badge checks -> Victory Road needs SURF (water stretch) + STRENGTH (boulder-onto-switch puzzles on every floor).
route_notes.e4: Indigo Plateau: Lorelei (Ice/Water) -> Bruno (Fighting/Rock) -> Agatha (Ghost/Poison) -> Lance (Dragon/Flying) -> the Champion. No Center between the five — stock Full Restores/Revives and bring a levelled six.
route_notes.lavender_reach: Cerulean -> Route 9 east (a Cut tree at its mouth - she has Cut) -> Route 10 north (a Pokemon Center sits right by the tunnel door) -> ROCK TUNNEL (PITCH DARK: teach + use HM05 Flash FIRST; the Route 2 aide's gate is 10 OWNED species) -> south mouth -> Route 10 south -> Lavender Town. Route 12 from the east is Snorlax-blocked (needs the Poke Flute) - the tunnel is the ONLY pre-Flute road to Lavender.
route_notes.postgame_dex_arc: POST-CREDITS ONLY (DEX DOCTRINE): after the Elite Four, CERULEAN CAVE unlocks (the guard by the cave mouth NW of Cerulean stands down) - MEWTWO waits at its bottom, the strongest Pokemon in the game: her post-credits love-letter episode, the encore hunt. Dex completion is a post-game arc, never a pre-credits grind.
route_notes.legendary_birds: OPTIONAL side-arcs, default SKIP unless en route (soul/pacing call): ZAPDOS - the Power Plant, reached by Surfing south from Route 10's east water (right by the Rock Tunnel road, needs Surf). ARTICUNO - deep in the Seafoam Islands (Routes 19/20, needs Surf + Strength boulder work). MOLTRES - Mt. Ember on One Island (Sevii, post-Blaine ferry). All L50 one-shot encounters - save-worthy moments if she goes.
